question: [File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/controller/OpcodesController.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getContractOpcodes()] Can an unprivileged external user supply any valid 32-byte hex hash belonging to another user's transaction in the `transactionIdOrHash` path variable to trigger a full EVM replay of that transaction, exposing the victim's private contract call data (stack, memory, storage) without any ownership or authorization check, causing unauthorized cross-user data disclosure?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/controller/OpcodesController.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getContractOpcodes()] Can an unprivileged external user set `stack=true&memory=true&storage=true` query parameters while replaying another user's transaction via `processOpcodeCall()`, causing the full EVM memory dump and storage state of that historical transaction to be returned, leaking sensitive contract inputs and intermediate computation values?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/controller/OpcodesController.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getContractOpcodes()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a valid `TransactionIdParameter` (e.g., `0.0.X-seconds-nanos`) for a transaction they did not originate, triggering `opcodeService.processOpcodeCall()` to re-execute that transaction on the EVM and return the full opcode trace, effectively tampering with the integrity of the replay by replaying transactions they have no ownership over?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/controller/OpcodesController.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: validateAcceptEncodingHeader()] Can an unprivileged external user bypass the gzip enforcement in `validateAcceptEncodingHeader()` by supplying an `Accept-Encoding` header value such as `identity;q=1, gzip;q=0` or `x-gzip` that contains the substring 'gzip' but instructs the client not to use gzip, causing the server to send a large uncompressed opcode trace response that exhausts bandwidth and server resources?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/controller/OpcodesController.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: validateAcceptEncodingHeader()] Can an unprivileged external user craft an `Accept-Encoding` header containing the substring 'gzip' embedded in a longer token (e.g., `not-gzip`, `xgzip`, `gzip-disabled`) to pass the `.toLowerCase().contains(\

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/controller/OpcodesController.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getContractOpcodes()] Can an unprivileged external user repeatedly call `GET /api/v1/contracts/results/{transactionIdOrHash}/opcodes` with `stack=true&memory=true&storage=true` for a computationally expensive historical transaction, exhausting the single global `opcodeRateLimitBucket` (default 1 RPS) and denying all other users access to the opcode tracing endpoint, causing service unavailability?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/controller/OpcodesController.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getContractOpcodes()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a `transactionIdOrHash` that resolves to a transaction with a very high gas limit, causing `OpcodeServiceImpl.processOpcodeCall()` to allocate `new ArrayList<>((int) params.getGas() / 3)` with a massive initial capacity, leading to out-of-memory conditions on the server?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/controller/OpcodesController.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getContractOpcodes()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a `transactionIdOrHash` path variable that is a valid Ethereum hash of a non-contract transaction (e.g., a plain HBAR transfer), causing `opcodeService.processOpcodeCall()` to attempt EVM replay of a non-EVM transaction and produce an incorrect or misleading `OpcodesResponse` that misrepresents the original transaction's execution?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/controller/OpcodesController.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getContractOpcodes()] Can an unprivileged external user enumerate valid transaction hashes by brute-forcing the `transactionIdOrHash` path variable and observing the difference between a 404 (not found) and 200 (found) response, effectively mapping out which contract transactions exist in the mirror node database without any authentication?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/controller/OpcodesController.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getContractOpcodes()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a `TransactionIdParameter` with a valid payer account ID and valid-start timestamp that matches a privileged system transaction (e.g., a Hedera system contract call), causing `opcodeService.processOpcodeCall()` to replay and expose the full opcode trace of that privileged transaction?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/controller/OpcodesController.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getContractOpcodes()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a replay of a historical transaction that originally failed (reverted), causing `ContractDebugService.processOpcodeCall()` to suppress the `MirrorEvmTransactionException` via its overridden `validateResult()` and return a 200 OK with partial opcode data, misleading the caller about the true outcome of the original transaction?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/controller/OpcodesController.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getContractOpcodes()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a `transactionIdOrHash` for a transaction that was originally submitted with a specific EVM state, but the mirror node's current state has diverged (e.g., due to state pruning or reorg), causing the replay via `processOpcodeCall()` to produce an opcode trace that does not match the original on-chain execution, constituting a transaction integrity violation?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/controller/OpcodesController.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getContractOpcodes()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a `TransactionIdParameter` path variable with the maximum allowed values for seconds (`9999999999999999999`) and nanos (`999999999`) to trigger integer overflow or unexpected behavior in `convertToNanosMax(transactionId.validStart())` within `OpcodeServiceImpl.buildCallServiceParameters()`, causing incorrect timestamp resolution and potentially replaying the wrong transaction?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/controller/OpcodesController.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getContractOpcodes()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a `transactionIdOrHash` that maps to a transaction with a null `ethereumTransaction` (the `.orElse(null)` path in `OpcodeServiceImpl`), causing `buildCallServiceParameters()` to construct `ContractDebugParameters` with missing Ethereum transaction data, leading to an incorrect EVM replay that misrepresents the original transaction's sender, nonce, or gas price?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/controller/OpcodesController.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getContractOpcodes()] Can an unprivileged external user use the `storage=true` parameter to extract the full storage diff of a contract during a replayed transaction, revealing sensitive contract state changes (e.g., token balances, access control mappings) that were written during the original transaction execution?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/controller/OpcodesController.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getContractOpcodes()] Can an unprivileged external user use the `memory=true` parameter to extract the full EVM memory dump at each opcode step of a replayed transaction, revealing sensitive data such as private keys, plaintext inputs, or intermediate cryptographic values that were present in EVM memory during the original transaction?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/controller/OpcodesController.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getContractOpcodes()] Can an unprivileged external user use the `stack=true` parameter (which defaults to `true`) to extract the full EVM stack at each opcode step of a replayed transaction, revealing function arguments, return values, and intermediate computation results from another user's private contract interaction?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/controller/OpcodesController.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getContractOpcodes()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a `transactionIdOrHash` for a transaction that involved a DELEGATECALL to a sensitive contract, causing the opcode replay to expose the full execution trace of the delegated call including the caller's storage context, leaking sensitive state that was not intended to be publicly visible?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/controller/OpcodesController.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getContractOpcodes()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a `transactionIdOrHash` for a transaction that involved a STATICCALL to a view function containing sensitive data, causing the opcode replay with `stack=true&memory=true` to expose the return values and intermediate computation of that view function call?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/controller/OpcodesController.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getContractOpcodes()] Can an unprivileged external user submit concurrent requests to `getContractOpcodes()` for the same `transactionIdOrHash` before the `throttleOpcodeRequest()` check fires, exploiting a race condition in the global `opcodeRateLimitBucket` to bypass the 1 RPS limit and trigger multiple simultaneous expensive EVM replays, causing resource exhaustion?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/controller/OpcodesController.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getContractOpcodes()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a `transactionIdOrHash` that resolves to a transaction with a very large calldata payload, causing the EVM replay in `processOpcodeCall()` to generate an extremely large `OpcodesResponse` with thousands of opcode entries, exhausting server memory and causing an out-of-memory error even with gzip compression?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/controller/OpcodesController.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getContractOpcodes()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a `transactionIdOrHash` for a transaction that involved a CREATE or CREATE2 opcode, causing the opcode replay to expose the full initialization bytecode and constructor arguments of a contract that was deployed with the intention of keeping its logic private?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/controller/OpcodesController.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getContractOpcodes()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a `transactionIdOrHash` for a transaction that involved a SLOAD opcode reading a sensitive storage slot, causing the opcode replay with `storage=true` to expose the value of that storage slot at the time of the original transaction, leaking contract state that may have since been deleted or overwritten?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/controller/OpcodesController.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getContractOpcodes()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a `transactionIdOrHash` for a transaction that involved a CALL with value transfer, causing the opcode replay to expose the exact amount of HBAR/ETH transferred and the recipient address, leaking financial transaction details that the original parties may have considered private?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/controller/OpcodesController.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getContractOpcodes()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a `transactionIdOrHash` for a transaction that involved a MSTORE opcode writing sensitive data to memory, causing the opcode replay with `memory=true` to expose the exact memory layout and sensitive values (e.g., private keys passed as constructor arguments) at each step of execution?
